title: Warm, Smoked Peruvian Chicken Pollo a la Brasa
description: A garlicky, shattering, light Peruvian dish featuring casabe and papillote.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together garlic, black pepper, chili flakes, and salt.
Rub the garlic mixture all over the chicken, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Place the chicken in the center of a piece of papillote, and add a few pieces of crushed casabe around it.
Brush the edges of the papillote with olive oil, then fold the paper over the chicken to create a tight packet.
Place the packets on the grill, and smoke for 45 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the heat, and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve the chicken with a squeeze of lemon juice and a sprinkle of crushed casabe.
For an authentic touch, serve the chicken on a wooden skewer, garnished with a sprig of cilantro or a slice of lime.
